# Ecommerce Workspace Instructions

- Build a production-oriented ecommerce monorepo with Next.js frontend and ASP.NET Core backend.
- Keep changes aligned with Clean Architecture, CQRS, MediatR, Repository Pattern, Unit of Work, EF Core, Dapper, SQL Server, Redis, JWT auth, role-based access, and Cloudinary image uploads.
- Frontend must use Next.js 15, React 19, TypeScript, TailwindCSS, shadcn-style components, Framer Motion, React Query, Zustand, and Axios.
- Prefer minimal, premium, mobile-first UI with light/dark mode, skeleton states, responsive layouts, and accessible controls.
- Backend layers must remain separated: Domain, Application, Infrastructure, API.
- Use DTOs, validators, handlers, repositories, controllers, global errors, pagination, filtering, logging, and Swagger.
- Do not expose admin UI to customers; protect admin routes and APIs with JWT roles.
- Keep code clean, focused, typed, and production-minded.
